author: w3Lb
content: Plan: whole-buffer splice path in engine::apply for eligible Replaces — O(input+matches) instead of per-line String allocation for every line. ELIGIBILITY (must be provably byte-identical to the line path): op=Replace, literal only (incl. case-insensitive literal — escaped pattern can't gain \n; REGEX EXCLUDED in v1 because \s/[^x]/\W/(?s) can match across line boundaries and textual analysis is unsound), find contains no \n/\r, no range, count != first_per_line (FirstInFile/Max via span truncation), and UNIFORM line endings (mixed-endings files take the line path because it normalizes via majority vote and splice wouldn't). Change metadata stays LINE-SHAPED for consumer compatibility: spans grouped by containing line via memchr scans (O(matches)), before/after reconstructed per affected line, line numbers counted incrementally. Also: context attached to at most the first 1000 changes per file in BOTH paths (documented; a 1M-change JSON response carrying 6M context lines helps nobody). KEY TEST: equivalence property — a range of 1..EOF forces the line path with identical semantics, so prop-compare splice vs apply-with-full-range on text+changes. Adds memchr dep to ripsed-core.
